data.title: U.S. Natural Gas Gains Ahead of Inventory Data — Market Talk
data.description: U.S. natural gas futures rise as weather forecasts turn colder for the last few days of November and the market banks on a colder start to December. The EIA's weekly inventory report due Thursday is expected to show the first storage withdrawal of the season. Analysts in a Wall Street Journal survey expect a 14 Bcf draw, reducing the surplus over the five-year average to 146 Bcf from 172 Bcf the week before. A smaller surplus and wider deficit to a year-ago "fits a market transitioning from injections to sustained withdrawals," Gelber & Associates says in a note. Nymex natural gas settles up 4.1%, at $4.55/mmBtu.(anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

data.title: Block's stock has suffered in a 'dismal' fintech market. Can these new numbers change its course?
data.description:
By Emily Bary

The parent company of Square and Cash App offers an upbeat long-term earnings forecast as it hosts its first investor day since 2022

Block operates the Square business, which sells payment technology to restaurants and stores.

The financial-technology sector has struggled to live up to its pandemic-era heyday, and that has meant some pain for Block investors recently.

Shares of Block (XYZ) have lost 12% over the past three years, as the S&P 500 SPX has advanced 67%. They've also lost 71% over five years, while the S&P 500 has climbed 86%.

"No matter what Block has tried over a fairly sustained period of time, it simply has not been resonating with investors," Seaport Research analyst Jeff Campbell wrote after the company's last earnings report. "What the 'scoreboard' clearly shows is [Block's] performance over the past three years is nothing to write home about - and its five-year performance is flat-out ugly to us," though it admittedly has been affected by general pressure on fintech valuations since the pandemic.

Now Block, the parent company of Square and Cash App, is trying once again to reset the narrative, hosting its first investor day since 2022 and putting fresh numbers around its financial opportunity.

While the FactSet consensus doesn't have estimates for all of the 2028 projections Block provided, it does have them for some, such as adjusted earnings per share. Block is modeling $5.50 on that metric, while analysts had been expecting just $4.76.

Block is also calling for about $15.8 billion in gross profit by 2028, reflecting a mid-teens annual growth rate until then, as well as upwards of $4 billion in adjusted cash flow, which the company expects to represent about 25% of gross profit within three years.

Over a shorter span, Block expects $11.98 billion in 2026 gross profit, along with $3.20 in adjusted earnings per share. The FactSet consensus calls for $11.78 billion and $3.24, respectively.

"We don't think our stock price today reflects the strength of the underlying fundamentals across our business," Chief Financial Officer Amrita Ahuja told MarketWatch.

She said Block has become a more "mature" company in the past three years, with a greater focus on profits and organizational structure. The company has emphasized cost efficiencies, sought to improve product development through artificial intelligence and deployed co-founder and CEO Jack Dorsey in a more hands-on role within the Square merchant business.

The Square unit, which offers payment technology to merchants, has been a source of consternation lately, partly as volume growth has outpaced gross-profit growth, raising questions about the profitability of incremental business.

Read on: Block's third-quarter results top expectations, but a few nitpicks weigh on the stock

Ahuja, though, said the discrepancy relates more to "transitory" factors than underlying business issues. The company has made upfront hardware investments that it expects to earn back over time as customers on its platform increase, and it's been experiencing higher processing costs with one of its partners but expects that impact to fade in the second quarter of 2026.

"If you normalize for those elements, what you'll see from [the first to the second to the third quarter] this year is actually accelerating gross-profit growth in each of those quarters, back in line or even slightly ahead of [gross payment volume] by the time you get to [the third quarter]," she said.

William Blair analyst Andrew Jeffrey spied opportunity for Square, as rival Clover, which is part of the beleaguered Fiserv (FISV), has leveraged pricing to drive what he thinks is unsustainable revenue growth.

"It is our view that Square will benefit from the competitive vacuum likely created by Clover's ... recent market pullback," he wrote ahead of the investor day.

See more: Fiserv's stock plunges 40% on worst day ever. Outlook reset is deemed 'difficult to comprehend.'

Ahuja said Square has seen new customers come on to its platform, which could make 2025 the strongest year for additional volume in the company's history.

"The core question is how is Square positioned competitively and to deliver value for our customers, and we think that there is a tremendous runway for growth for Square," she said.

Block is also focused on driving synergies between its businesses - not just Square and the Cash App peer-to-peer payment service, but also Afterpay, the buy-now-pay-later platform that's been integrated across various Block offerings. The company has become more "interconnected," Ahuja said.

Additionally, Block is boosting its share-buyback program by $5 billion. As of Sept. 30, it had $1.1 billion left from a prior $4 billion authorization.

"We generally do not consider analyst days as positive catalysts and acknowledge that Block has a high bar to clear as it seeks to re-engage investors in a dismal fintech market," Jeffrey wrote ahead of the event. "However, we believe this event could drive shares considering sour sentiment."

Don't miss: Visa, Mastercard reach new settlement with merchants. Will it shake up credit-card rewards?

-Emily Bary

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: US - Ara Partners buys stake in Centric Fiber
data.description:
By Alison Healey

Private equity and infrastructure investment firm Ara Partners has acquired a majority controlling stake in Houston-based Centric Fiber, a bulk fibre-to-the-home platform in the southern US. Financial details were not revealed.

The deal was completed in partnership with Centric Fiber’s management team. Centric Fiber partners with developers and builders to provide fibre services to residential customers across Texas, Florida, and Arizona. Ara Infrastructure has committed additional capital to support the execution of Centric's pipeline of contracted projects and backlog, as well as new growth opportunities.

Ara was advised by Kirkland & Ellis and RBC Capital Markets. Centric was advised by Simpson Thacher & Bartlett and Guggenheim Securities.

data.title: PLATTS: 100--Florida Gas, REX file pipeline expansion applications with US FERC
data.description: (Platts)--19Nov2025/329 pm EST/2029 GMT **REX seeks new lateral in Wyoming **Florida Gas plans to serve multiple customers Florida Gas Transmission and Rockies Express Pipeline filed separate applications with federal regulators to expand their systems to transport more gas for power generation and other needs. The REX project would enable transportation of 419,000 Dt/d to a new delivery point and short pipeline lateral in Laramie County, Wyoming, for a planned cogeneration power plant and fuel cell power plant. REX, which is owned by Tallgrass Energy, sought authorization from the US Federal Energy Regulatory Commission to build a 7.2-mile lateral of 36-inch-diameter pipe and related facilities. A new meter station and a new interconnect for the Switchgrass lateral on the REX system are planned, according to the Nov. 17 application (CP26-28). The project is estimated to cost $57.3 million. REX is looking to begin construction of the lateral and related facilities in April 2026 to meet a planned in-service date in October 2026. The end-users for the gas are BFC Power and Cheyenne Power Hub, which are developing the cogeneration and fuel cell power plants for an industrial park, REX said. BFC Power is a subsidiary of Tallgrass, and it recently applied to the Wyoming Department of Environmental Quality for an air permit to add up to 900 MW of Bloom Energy fuel cells in Laramie County. The proposed power plants are likely related to Tallgrass' 1.8-GW data center project in Wyoming in partnership with infrastructure developer Crusoe, though the application at FERC does not indicate that. "The project will ensure a reliable supply of fuel gas to the planned power generation facility, enabling it to directly provide power to its industrial customers and mitigate the burden of the industrial customers' load on the local power grid," REX told FERC. A spokesperson for Tallgrass did not immediately respond to questions on Nov. 19 about the pipeline project and power generation plans. Both the REX application and the Florida Gas application were made under the companies' blanket certificates, which enable quicker reviews by FERC when no protests are filed. The commission in late June raised the financial cost threshold for projects that qualify for blanket certificate reviews, enabling more pipeline projects to be filed under those criteria. Through the blanket certificate regulations, pipelines notify FERC of relatively small facility changes that are planned. After an environmental review by FERC staff, and if no party protests a filing, FERC approval is deemed granted, and construction could start sooner than through a Natural Gas Act Section 7 application process. FLORIDA GAS EXPANSION Florida Gas listed several shippers that have signed firm transportation agreements for its South Central Alabama Project (CP26-26). The project involves small changes to mainline, lateral and other facilities on the Florida Gas system to meet demand for 170,000 MMBtu/d, with delivery points in Mobile, Baldwin and Escambia counties, Alabama. The project has residential, commercial, and industrial customers, including recycling and manufacturing plant use of the added gas capacity, along with power generation, Florida Gas said. The Florida Gas facility changes include pipeline looping in Mississippi, Alabama and Florida, with new tap valves on laterals and some other modifications. About 1.3 miles of 42-inch-diameter mainline pipe would be looped in Mississippi and Alabama, with nearly 2 miles of 36-inch-diameter pipe looped in Santa Rosa County, Florida. The precedent agreements signed by all the shippers, at negotiated rates, are dependent upon the facilities being in service on May 1, 2026, or earlier, Florida Gas said. The project includes reallocating 25,000 MMBtu/d of existing capacity for use by NextEra Energy utility subsidiary Florida Power & Light, in addition to 50,000 MMBtu/d of incremental capacity to be used by FPL. The utility would take delivery of 75,000 MMBtu/d in Escambia County under an agreement with an initial term of five years, according to the Nov. 17 application. Another expansion shipper, ArcelorMittal Calvert, a steel processing facility in Calvert, Alabama, signed an agreement with a 20-year term for 45,000 MMBtu/d. Other expansion shippers are PowerSouth Energy Cooperative, at 25,000 MMBtu/d; aluminum manufacturer Novelis, at 15,000 MMBtu/d; and the Clarke-Mobile County Gas District, at 10,000 MMBtu/d. All those agreements have 20-year terms. Florida Gas held an open season in May to solicit interest in a pipeline expansion and any existing capacity to be turned back by existing shippers, it told FERC. No requests to turn back capacity were received, and the precedent agreements signed by the expansion shippers were the result of the open season, the company said. The project has an estimated cost of $39 million. — Thomas Tiernan, thomas.tiernan@spglobal.com-- Edited by Michael Lustig, newsdesk@spglobal.com--Platts Electricity Alert--

data.title: New Zealand's a2 Milk lifts fiscal 2026 results forecast
data.description:
New Zealand's a2 Milk on Thursday raised its full-year earnings forecast, citing stronger-than-expected trading in its infant milk formula, or IMF, along with a weaker New Zealand dollar that is expected to boost its top line.

The Auckland-headquartered firm said trading in IMF, its top market, was stronger than expected, with English-label IMF revenue growth forecast to outpace China-label IMF growth.

IMF is the company's core product category, primarily targeting babies and toddlers, and contributed 67% of total sales revenue in fiscal 2025, according to its annual report.

The dairy company now expects low-double-digit revenue growth from continuing operations in fiscal 2026, higher than its previous forecast of high-single-digit growth, and anticipates slightly higher net profit after tax than last year.

Earlier in August, the company announced its fiscal 2025 results and logged a group revenue from continuing operations of NZ$1.76 billion ($984.02 million), while it recorded a 21% jump in its NPAT to NZ$202.9 million.

Trading was also stronger in its other nutritionals and liquid milk product segments, a2 Milk added.

Its nutritionals portfolio also comprises non-IMF powdered a2 Milk products, as well as China & other Asia liquid milk products.

The company said movements in actual and forecast currency rates would inflate revenue and expenses, but the net impact on EBITDA, after hedge losses, was not expected to be material.

It also expects to spend in the range of NZ$60 million to NZ$80 million for fiscal 2026, higher than what it had flagged in August.

The firm said in August that it would buy a nutritional manufacturing facility in New Zealand from a unit of China Mengniu Dairy for NZ$282 million, a move aimed at boosting the company's presence in its top market, China.

($1 = 1.7886 New Zealand dollars)

data.title: PREVIEW: Walmart seen posting nearly 4% Q3 EPS growth in test of consumer resiliency
data.description:
** Shares of Walmart off about 1% ahead of the company's results due before the bell on Thursday, as investors eye consumer health

** Retail giant expected to post adj EPS of $0.60, or 3.6% higher than Q3 2024, on revenue of $177.43 bln vs $169.59 bln a year ago, according to LSEG data

** Co missed EPS estimates last qtr, but has beaten estimates over remaining eight most recent qtrs

** On Nov 14, CEO Doug McMillon announced he'll retire next year, capping more than a decade at the helm of the world's largest retailer

** In its last report, despite EPS disappointment, WMT showed revenue boost on strong consumer demand across the spectrum of income levels

** Of the 44 analysts covering the stock, 43 rate "strong buy" or "buy", while one recommends "hold"

** Median PT of $115 is approx 15% above current levels

** Including move on the session, WMT shares up ~11% YTD compared with the Dow's ~8.5% gain and S&P 500 Consumer Staples sector's ~0.3% decline

data.title: Dolphin Drilling: Blackford contract extended
data.description:
Dolphin Drilling AS (Dolphin Drilling, OSE: DDRIL) announced today that Oil India Limited has exercised its option to extend its drilling contract with the deepwater capable semisubmersible rig, Blackford Dolphin. Further to the initial 3 well firm campaign over an estimated 14 month duration disclosed in the company’s press release of 14th March 2024, the rig will now continue operations for an additional 120 days through to 10 May 2026 to complete drilling, testing, and abandonment work at locations East of India. The extended term of the scope of work is based on the same rate and terms as the existing contract. 

Dolphin Drilling is pleased to continue its partnership with Oil India Limited and remains committed to delivering safe, efficient and sustainable operations.

For further information, please contact: Ingolf Gillesdal, CFO, tel: +47 920 45 320

Dolphin Drilling | www.dolphindrilling.com

Dolphin Drilling AS is an Oslo listed, Aberdeen head-quartered, company which owns and operates a fleet of harsh environment mid-water & deep-water semisubmersible drilling rigs, capable of working worldwide.

EQUITIES

GLOBAL - The Nasdaq rose on Wednesday, clawing back some ground lost during the recent selloff as investors positioned themselves ahead of Nvidia's NVDA.O much-anticipated quarterly results and crucial employment data that had been unavailable during the longest-ever U.S. government shutdown.

MSCI's gauge of stocks across the globe EURONEXT:IACWI fell 2.07 points, or 0.21%, to 973.94.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

NEW YORK - The S&P 500 and Nasdaq edged higher in volatile trading on Wednesday as technology stocks rose ahead of Nvidia's quarterly results, while the Dow eased.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 53.77 points, or 0.12%, to 46,038.42, the S&P 500 gained 14.54 points, or 0.22%, to 6,631.86 and the Nasdaq Composite gained 95.36 points, or 0.42%, to 22,527.61.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

LONDON - European shares closed flat on Wednesday while investors looked ahead to a high-stakes earnings report from chipmaker NvidiaNVDA.O, which could set the direction for trading in artificial intelligence-related stocks.

The pan-European STOXX 600 ended flat at 561.71 points.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

TOKYO - Japan's Nikkei share gauge lost ground for a fourth day on Wednesday as investors remained wary ahead of pivotal earnings from artificial intelligence heavyweight Nvidia NVDA.O.

The Nikkei 225 Index swung between gains and losses before closing down 0.3% to 48,537.70, marking its longest losing streak in seven weeks.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

SHANGHAI - Hong Kong stocks fell for a fourth straight session to a two-week low on Wednesday, following an overnight drop on Wall Street, while China shares rebounded.

In mainland China, the blue-chip CSI300 Index climbed 0.4%, while the Shanghai Composite Index bounced 0.2%.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

AUSTRALIA - Australian shares were likely to inch higher at open on Thursday, with mining stocks expected to spearhead gains on steady underlying commodity pricing, while potential losses in energy stocks due to lower oil prices could temper broad gains.

The local share price index futures (YAPcm1) rose 0.4%, a 50.1-point premium to the underlying S&P/ASX 200 index close. The benchmark fell 0.3% on Wednesday.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

SEOUL - South Korean shares fell for a second straight session on Wednesday, as chipmakers dropped ahead of U.S. artificial intelligence firm Nvidia's quarterly results.

The benchmark KOSPI closed down 24.11 points, or 0.61%, at 3,929.51, after falling more than 3% on Tuesday.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

FOREIGN EXCHANGE

NEW YORK - The dollar rose against the yen on Wednesday, with the Japanese currency falling to a 10-month low after Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama said Japan's new government was closely monitoring markets.

The dollar index , which measures the greenback's strength against a basket of six currencies, edged 0.45% higher at 100.03.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

CHINA - China's yuan was little changed against the dollar on Wednesday, with traders cautious ahead of key U.S. jobs data that could sway the Federal Reserve's December policy stance and the dollar's direction.

By 0320 GMT, the yuan was 0.04% lower at 7.1100 to the dollar after trading in a range of 7.1081 to 7.1133.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

AUSTRALIA - The Australian and New Zealand dollars fell on Wednesday amid investor caution ahead of results from artificial intelligence bellwether Nvidia that are likely to have an outsized impact on global risk sentiment.

The Aussie declined 0.3% to $0.6491 to reverse a small gain from overnight.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

SEOUL - The South Korean won weakened against the U.S. dollar.

The won was quoted at 1,465.6 per dollar on the onshore settlement platform , 0.25% lower than its previous close at 1,461.9.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

TREASURIES

NEW YORK - Treasury yields rose on Wednesday after the U.S. government said jobs data for October and November will not come out before the Federal Reserve's December meeting, leading traders to reduce bets on an interest rate cut that month.

The yield on benchmark U.S. 10-year notes rose 0.4 basis points to 4.127%.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

LONDON - Euro zone 10-year yields held near multi-week highs on Wednesday as investors eyed stock markets to see if a fresh selloff could trigger a rush into government bonds.

Germany's 10-year yield (DE10YT=RR), the euro area's benchmark, was flat at 2.71% after hitting 2.718% early this week, its highest since October 7.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

TOKYO - Japanese government bonds (JGBs) slid on Wednesday, sending benchmark yields to a 17-year high, as concerns swirled about the size of Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's stimulus package.

The 10-year JGB yield (JP10YTN=JBTC) rose 1.5 basis points (bps) to 1.76%, and earlier reached 1.775%, the highest since June 2008.

For a full report, click on

COMMODITIES

GOLD

Gold prices pared gains on Wednesday after the release of minutes from the Federal Reserve's latest meeting, while market participants focused on upcoming economic data for further clues on the U.S. interest rate path.

Spot gold XAU= was up 0.2% at $4,073.79 per ounce, at 2:25 p.m. ET (1925 GMT), after climbing over 1% earlier in the session.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

IRON ORE

Iron ore futures prices rose on Wednesday to their highest in more than two weeks, helped by firm demand in top consumer China and falling domestic supply.

The most-traded January iron ore contract on China's Dalian Commodity Exchange (DCE) closed daytime trade 0.76% higher at 791.5 yuan ($111.32) a metric ton, after touching its highest since November 3 at 797 yuan earlier in the session.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

BASE METALS

Copper prices recovered on Wednesday as some investors viewed the recent retreat a good entry point as stock markets stabilise amid persistent supply concerns.

Benchmark three-month copper on the London Metal Exchange was up 0.4% at $10,764.50 a metric ton by 1730 GMT.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

OIL

Oil prices fell on Wednesday after reports indicated the United States is renewing its push to end Russia's war in Ukraine and has drafted a framework for it.

Brent crude futures fell $1.63, or 2.5%, to $63.26 a barrel 12:15 p.m. ET (1715 GMT), while U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude futures were down $1.56, or 2.6%, to $59.18.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

PALM OIL

Malaysian palm oil futures rose to their highest close in almost three weekson Wednesday after rising for five straight sessions, supported by stronger soyoil prices.

The benchmark palm oil contract for January delivery on the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives Exchange gained 17 ringgit, or 0.4%, to 4,226 ringgit ($1,018.80) a metric ton at the close.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

RUBBER

Japanese rubber futures rose for a ninth consecutive session on Wednesday, underpinned by supply concerns due to weather risks in southern Thailand, the world's largest producer.

The Osaka Exchange (OSE) rubber contract for April delivery , was up 5.3 yen, or 1.62%, at 331.9 yen($2.14) per kg.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

data.title: Turners Automotive Group Ltd.: Two-Speed Economy is Expected to Persist into Calendar Year 2026
Red Post Energy Announces Madhivanan T. A. ("Madhi") as Advisory Board Member, Strengthening Global Infrastructure & Data-Center Expertise
data.description:
HOUSTON, Nov. 19, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — Red Post Energy is proud to announce the appointment of Madhivanan T. A. ("Madhi") to its Advisory Board. A strategic global leader with more than 30 years of experience across energy, chemicals, oil & gas, automotive, and technology sectors, Madhi brings unparalleled expertise in building resilient, scalable, and high-performance infrastructure worldwide.

In his current role as Director of Global Supply Chain at Microsoft, Madhi leads mission-critical data-center infrastructure delivery across the Eastern United States and oversees power-infrastructure execution throughout Latin America. He manages multi-billion-dollar capital programs that underpin the digital economy, ensuring accelerated timelines, capital efficiency, and robust supplier ecosystems that support global scaling efforts.

Madhi is recognized for his disciplined execution of Giga Projects, mastery of long-lead global supply chains, and commitment to developing diverse, high-performing teams. His work ensures that the world's largest technology platforms have the stable, reliable power and operational resilience they need to meet soaring data, AI, and cloud-infrastructure demands.

Prior to Microsoft, Madhi spent more than a decade at DOW Inc., where he delivered mega-projects and turnaround programs across Europe, LATAM, Asia, and the Middle East, strengthening global capital execution, scenario planning, and operational risk management. His earlier leadership roles at Reliance Industries, Maruti Suzuki, and Honda further expanded his operational and manufacturing depth across multiple continents.

A PMP-certified executive, ICF-certified coach, and Harvard-trained board leader, Madhi blends rigorous governance with a thoughtful, people-centered leadership philosophy. His global perspective and strategic insight align directly with Red Post Energy's mission of building long-life, high-capacity power infrastructure that supports both national energy security and the growing demands of next-generation technologies.

"Madhi's experience leading global supply chains for one of the world's most influential technology companies brings invaluable perspective to Red Post Energy," said Lance Medlin, President & Founder of Red Post Energy. "His understanding of complex capital execution, power-infrastructure delivery, and AI-driven data-center growth will help guide our strategy as we build the energy backbone of America's future."

Madhi's appointment reflects Red Post Energy's commitment to assembling top-tier, globally experienced leadership to drive innovation in energy production, grid stability, data-center power solutions, and national-security-aligned infrastructure.

Media Contact

Lance Medlin

CEO & Founder

Red Post Energy

Media@redpostenergy.com

+1 (713) 575-8307

SOURCE Red Post Energy

data.title: CBOT soybeans extend retreat after Chinese buying sparked gains
data.description:
Chicago Board of Trade soybean futures closed lower on Wednesday as traders sought to gauge the strength of Chinese buying, following the trade truce between Washington and Beijing last month.

  • CBOT January soybeans (SF26) ended 17-1/4 cents lower at $11.36-1/4 per bushel.

  • CBOT December soymeal (SMZ25) closed down $8.10 at $318.90 per short ton, while December soyoil (BOZ25) slid 1.07 cent to 51.10 cents per pound.

  • The soybean market has retreated after rallying on Tuesday to its highest level since June 2024.

  • soy for shipment in December and January. Then, the U.S. soybeans to China on Wednesday.

  • Traders were waiting for the U.S. Department of Agriculture to release U.S. grain and soy export sales data on Thursday, after the agency halted the release of weekly reports during the federal government's shutdown.

  • For the week that ended on October 2, analysts estimated 2025-26 U.S. soybean export sales were 600,000 to 1.6 million metric tons, according to a Reuters poll.

data.title: ESALQ Cattle Analysis
data.description: cattle analysis CATTLE/CEPEA: Calf prices may continue to rise up to 26/27 transition Cepea, 19 - Price rises and price drops for calf throughout time end up defining what is known as the livestock cycle. Currently, calf prices are movingup, bringing questions about for how long they might continue on the rise. The CEPEA/ESALQ monthly calf price Index (Mato Grosso do Sul State) indicates that, from 2000 to October 2025 (values were deflated by the IGP-DI October/25),the periods of high prices last from 30 to 32 months. The last moment of price increases was from September/18 to April/21, when the Index rose 90% (in real terms). Current price rises for calf in Mato Grosso do Sul have been taking place since July 2024. Considering the historical data, the trend would be quotations moving up for more 13 or 15 months (totaling 30-32 months). Thus, the upward trend might occur up to late 2026. For the Portuguese version please refer to or click on <ESQ/CATL/ANALYS>

data.title: Clean Vision Corp SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Clean Vision Corp, a company specializing in sustainable waste management solutions, has released its Q3 10-Q report, showcasing significant financial and operational developments. The report highlights the company's performance in Morocco and outlines future expansion plans, particularly in the United States.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $125,201, reflecting an increase from $107,946 in the prior year, indicating growth in sales from the company's operations in Morocco.
  • Gross Margin: $105,900, showing an improvement from $96,682, suggesting better cost management and efficiency in operations.
  • Loss from Operations: $(4,174,916), compared to $(3,235,749) in the previous year, indicating increased operating expenses.
  • Net Loss: $(5,633,750), slightly improved from $(5,705,277) in the prior year, reflecting ongoing challenges in achieving profitability.
  • Loss per share - basic and diluted: $(0.01), consistent with the previous year, indicating no change in earnings per share despite operational challenges.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company's operations in Morocco generated approximately $125,000 in revenue from the sale of commodities, specifically from the provision of pyrolysis services and the sale of byproducts. This marks an increase from the previous year's revenue of $108,000 from the same operations.
  • Geographical Performance: The company's operations in Morocco have been a focal point, with the pyrolysis facility in Agadir having a capacity to convert 20 tons per day of waste plastic. The company is also planning to expand its operations in West Virginia, USA, with a facility expected to be operational in Q4 2025.
  • Sales Units: The Morocco facility is currently converting 20 tons per day of waste plastic through pyrolysis, showcasing the company's operational capacity in this region.
  • New Production Launches: Clean-Seas West Virginia is expected to begin operations in Q4 2025, initially converting 50 tons per day of plastic feedstock, with plans to expand to over 500 tons per day within three years.
  • Future Outlook: The company is focused on expanding its operations and increasing its production capacity. The West Virginia facility is expected to play a significant role in this expansion, with plans to scale up operations significantly over the next few years.

SEC Filing: Clean Vision Corp [ CLNV ] - 10-Q - Nov. 19, 2025

data.title: PLATTS: 100--Arizona regulators approve doc-less intervention in DOE, FERC large loads docket
data.description: (Platts)--19Nov2025/324 pm EST/2024 GMT **FERC required to take final action by April 30 **NARUC urges preservation of state authority The Arizona Corporation Commission agreed Nov. 19 to a document-less intervention related to a US Department of Energy letter to the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission to assert jurisdiction over the interconnection of large loads. The doc-less intervention allows the commission to show interest in the issue without filing a letter or comments into the docket. "It says, hey, we are interested in this issue," Commission Chair Kevin Thompson. "I think it's important that we stay abreast of this and understand what's going on. ... I do want to preserve our ability to have a seat at the table, and I just don't want to have to comment to FERC on any issues until we know the rules that we're playing by." All five commissioners agreed. "I think it's important that we do intervene," Commissioner Lea Marquez Peterson said. "This is such a hot controversial issue across the nation. It's important that Arizona has a voice in this conversation." DOE LETTER TO FERC The secretary's letter, which included a series of principles, requires FERC to consider the proposal and take final action by April 30, 2026. "This docket is basically to establish the principles and to begin the process to establish detailed interconnection requirements for large loads, which is something novel," Sean Bodkin, commission senior associate general counsel, said about the DOE's docket. "Loads have never been subject to jurisdiction in the past. It's always been generation and transmission. ... They're relying on the fact that this is an interconnected grid and that these large loads are relying on energy from possibly interstate commerce." In an Oct. 23 letter to FERC, the US Secretary of Energy proposed an advanced notice of proposed rulemaking on the interconnection of large loads to the interstate transmission system. "To usher in a new era of American prosperity, we must ensure all Americans and domestic industries have access to affordable, reliable and secure electricity," Secretary of Energy Chris Wright wrote in the letter to FERC. "To do this, large loads, including AI data centers, served by public utilities must be able to connect to the transmission system in a timely, orderly, and non-discriminatory manner. This is an urgent issue that requires prompt attention." While FERC historically has not exerted jurisdiction over load interconnections, "it is my view that the interconnection of large loads directly to the interstate transmission system to access the transmission system and the electricity transmitted over it falls squarely within the Commission's jurisdiction," Wright added. "Asserting Commission jurisdiction is in the public's interest." The National Association of Regulatory Utility Commissioners on Nov. 11 approved a resolution calling on FERC to preserve state retail authority when considering the proposed rule to greatly expand the federal government's role in the interconnection of data centers and other large loads. In separate actions, the commission approved certificates of environmental compatibility for the following: **West Valley Central Transmission Project **Harquahala North Transmission Line Project **Rio Sol To Saguaro Interconnect Project — Kassia Micek, kassia.micek@spglobal.com-- Edited by Giselle Rodriguez, newsdesk@spglobal.com--Platts Electricity Alert--

data.title: Goodman Property Trust 1H Net NZD61.8M
GE Vernova Shares Up After Taiwan Power Company Onshore Wind Deal
data.description:
By Terell Wright

Shares of GE Vernova rose after the company said it received its first international onshore wind repower upgrade agreement with Taiwan Power Company.

Shares climbed 6% to $587.53 and are up 73% year-to-date.

The power, wind and electrification company on Wednesday said it will provide Taiwan Power Company repower and upgrade kits for 25 GE Vernova turbines and deliver a five-year operations and maintenance services package.

GE Vernova said the project will modernize aging turbines, improving their performance and reliability, which will help the TPC produce more affordable, renewable power.

GE Vernova said it booked the order in the third quarter. Initial components are slated to be delivered this quarter, with retrofit installation taking place throughout 2026 and 2027.

Write to Terell Wright at terell.wright@wsj.com

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P 500 edge up ahead of Nvidia earnings; Dow down
data.description:
Key points:
  • Target drops after missing quarterly comparable sales estimates
  • Indexes slightly pare gains after Fed minutes
  • Indexes: Dow down 0.1%, S&P 500 up 0.2%, Nasdaq up 0.4%

By Caroline Valetkevitch

The S&P 500 and Nasdaq edged higher in volatile trading on Wednesday as technology stocks rose ahead of Nvidia's quarterly results, while the Dow eased.

Indexes pared gains after minutes from the last Federal Reserve meeting showed policymakers cautioned that lower borrowing costs could undermine the fight against inflation. The Fed cut rates by a quarter of a percentage point at each of its meetings in September and October.

Worries about a cooling labor market persisted ahead of Thursday's release of the September U.S. jobs report following the record-long U.S. government shutdown. The U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics said it will not publish the October employment report, but will instead combine nonfarm payrolls for that month with November's report.

"A lot has changed since the Fed last met, but it seems as though the biggest disagreement among Fed officials was the timing of the next move, not the magnitude or direction," said Brian Jacobsen, chief economist at Annex Wealth Management in Menomonee Falls, Wisconsin.

Shares of Nvidia were up 1.7%, with its earnings seen as a test for the artificial intelligence-driven rally that has pushed the market to record highs this year.

The stock is "almost guaranteed to be volatile," following the report, said Tim Ghriskey, senior portfolio strategist at Ingalls & Snyder in New York.

"When you're the biggest company out there, you're sort of in the headlights, and it often does not make sense how the stock reacts in the short term," he said.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 53.77 points, or 0.12%, to 46,038.42, the S&P 500 gained 14.54 points, or 0.22%, to 6,631.86 and the Nasdaq Composite gained 95.36 points, or 0.42%, to 22,527.61.

Investors have worried about how companies will be able to make money from their huge investments in AI.

Options data from analytics firm Option Research & Technology Services (ORATS) showed an implied move of about 7% for Nvidia's stock in either direction after its results.

Among decliners, Target shares slipped after the retailer reported a bigger-than-expected drop in quarterly sales with cash-strapped U.S. consumers pulling back on discretionary spending.

Declining issues outnumbered advancers by a 2.03-to-1 ratio on the NYSE, where there were 52 new highs and 186 new lows.

On the Nasdaq, 1,574 stocks rose and 3,006 fell as declining issues outnumbered advancers by a 1.91-to-1 ratio.
